one_line: Maharajadhiraja of Magadha and founder of the early Gupta power in the eastern Gangetic plain.

appearance.age_look: Mid-30s to late-30s, athletic medium build with a ruler’s calm poise.
appearance.face: Oval face with sharp jawline, medium-brown North Indian complexion, straight nose, dark expressive eyes under slightly arched brows, neatly kept short beard and moustache framing the mouth; default expression is composed and confident, with a hint of strategic intensity.
appearance.hair: Thick black hair gathered into a low, compact topknot at the back of the crown, sides smooth and tidy; a simple narrow gold circlet with a small central red gemstone sits low on the forehead, not tall like a later crown.
appearance.costume: Era-accurate Gupta royal attire: a finely woven off-white cotton uttariya (draped shawl) diagonally across the bare upper torso from left shoulder to right hip, bordered with a narrow red-and-gold stripe; a fitted knee-length reddish-maroon silk dhoti wrapped and pleated in front with a subtle golden floral motif near the hem, held by a plain broad gold waistbelt; simple leather sandals; jewellery limited to a short gold necklace of flat discs, a single gold armlet on the right arm with a stylized lotus motif, slim gold bangles on the left wrist, and small round gold ear studs; no heavy armour, only a light brown leather bracer on the left forearm hinting at practical readiness.
appearance.signature: A long, slightly curved iron sword with a plain bronze hilt and no guard, sheathed in a brown leather scabbard with a simple gold fitting, worn slung from his left hip by a dark red sash, often held in his left hand as a symbol of authority rather than aggression.

ref_desc: Chandragupta I the First is a mid-30s athletic North Indian king with medium-brown skin, oval face, short beard, black hair in a low topknot under a narrow gold circlet, wearing an off-white diagonal uttariya, reddish-maroon patterned dhoti, simple gold jewellery, leather sandals, and carrying a slightly curved iron sword with a plain bronze hilt in a brown scabbard, accented by off-white, reddish-maroon, gold, and dark tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of Chandragupta I the First, a mid-30s athletic North Indian king with a calm, confident expression, medium-brown complexion, oval face, straight nose, dark intense eyes, and a neatly trimmed short beard and moustache. His thick black hair is gathered into a low compact topknot at the back of the crown, with the sides smooth, and he wears a narrow gold circlet with a small red gemstone centered low on his forehead. Dress him in early 4th-century Gupta royal clothing: an off-white cotton uttariya draped diagonally from his left shoulder across his bare chest to his right hip, edged with a narrow red-and-gold border; a fitted knee-length reddish-maroon silk dhoti wrapped and pleated in front with a subtle golden floral motif near the hem, secured by a plain broad gold waistbelt; and simple leather sandals. Add restrained jewellery: a short gold necklace of flat discs, a single gold armlet with a lotus motif on his right arm, slim gold bangles on his left wrist, and small round gold ear studs, plus a light brown leather bracer on his left forearm to hint at practicality. His signature weapon is a long slightly curved iron sword with a plain bronze hilt and no guard, in a brown leather scabbard with a simple gold fitting, worn on his left hip by a dark red sash; show him either resting his left hand on the hilt or holding the sheathed sword lightly. Emphasize a dignified heroic stance, clear anime linework, and a consistent palette of off-white, reddish-maroon, gold, medium-brown skin tone, and dark hair and leather accents.
